extraction.one_line_summary: KRISTOFF Alexander won Stage 1 sprint finish in Nice by positioning opportunistically while expected favorites Bennett and Viviani were compromised by earlier crashes and chase efforts.
tactical_patterns: Wet descent after first climb fractured peloton badly enough that Greipel lost 2 minutes, revealing positioning pre-climb critical.
Bennett and Duviost crash on lap 1 forced chase efforts that likely compromised sprint legs on hard circuit.
Trek-Segafredo led out final kilometer but Kristoff exploited without dedicated train, showing sprinter autonomy advantage on complex finishes.

rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: Won despite not being original team selection, suggesting form peaked late and team underestimated his condition.
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: Crashed early, forced chase effort on hard circuit with climbs, visibly compromised in sprint (4th).
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Dropped twice during stage before finishing 6th, suggests climbing weakness on even moderate gradients problematic.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: 5th place consistent with green jersey hunting rather than stage win priority on hard circuit.
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: negative: Faded to 7th after being positioned, indicates either poor form or insufficient power on uphill finish.
